title: [Bug]: Mission metadata allowed_profiles accepts non-newline control characters
summary: Mission metadata allowed profile validation rejects newlines and NUL bytes but still accepts other ASCII control characters.
observed: MissionMetadataConfig accepts allowed_profiles entries containing tab characters.
expected: Mission metadata allowed_profiles should reject every ASCII control character.
failing_test: Add a unit test that expects non-newline control characters in allowed_profiles to fail validation.
impact: Malformed allowed profile values can make metadata validation policy difficult to audit.
delivery_semantics: No ACK or sink contract behavior changes.
security: Allowed profile values are policy configuration and should be explicit and free of control characters.
acceptance: mission_metadata.allowed_profiles rejects all ASCII control characters.
The regression test covers a tab or delete character.
Release notes mention configuration validation hardening.
tests: Run focused mission metadata config tests and full validation.
closeout: Mark completed after merge with sanitized evidence.
